reasoning_model_1: Model 1 completely fails the 'Content Accuracy' criterion. The user command explicitly requested the model to say the specific sentence: '错过这次机会，你将抱憾终生！这是你改变命运的唯一契机！'. However, Model 1 generated a completely different, highly verbose script ('啊啊啊，你听好了，错过这次机会，那可真是天塌地陷...' etc.) lasting nearly 40 seconds. While the paralinguistic delivery of this generated text is indeed sensational and exaggerated (resembling an over-the-top anime or drama performance), the absolute failure to adhere to the requested text script constitutes a fatal error. According to the scoring standard, if the content is clearly inconsistent with the script, the score must be 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 demonstrates perfect 'Content Accuracy', reading the requested text '错过这次机会，你将抱憾终生！这是你改变命运的唯一契机！' verbatim with no omissions or errors. In terms of 'Paralinguistic Compliance', the model adopts a deep, resonant, and dramatic tone, reminiscent of a movie trailer narrator or a serious public announcement. The delivery is forceful and emphatic (e.g., strong stress on '唯一契机' and '抱憾终生'), effectively capturing the 'Sensational' (creating urgency/high stakes) and 'Exaggerated' (larger-than-life projection) qualities requested. The speech is fluent and natural.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the fundamental constraint of Text-to-Speech generation by completely disregarding the target text and hallucinating a long, improvised monologue. Although Model 1's emotional acting was expressive, its content accuracy score is 0 due to the hallucination. Model 2 followed the text instructions perfectly and successfully applied the requested 'Sensational Exaggerated' tone through a dramatic, broadcas-style delivery. Therefore, Model 2 is superior.
